## Code Review

This pull request introduces code-splitting and lazy-loading optimizations for the AR Disc Golf application, moving heavy dependencies like MapLibre and AR.js/A-Frame into dynamic imports and separate vendor chunks. It also refactors the scorecard to only calculate scores and par for completed holes during active rounds. The review feedback highlights critical resource leaks, specifically a webcam stream leak on unmounting ARScene and unnecessary camera activity when the AR view is inactive. Additionally, the reviewer noted that failed dynamic imports of MapLibre are cached indefinitely, preventing subsequent retries, and provided suggestions to reset the load promise on rejection.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Using `arSceneEnabled` keeps the `<ARScene>` component mounted even when `showAr` is `false` (e.g., when the user is looking at the map). For `arjs` mode, this keeps the webcam stream running constantly in the background, causing severe battery drain. Mounting `<ARScene>` only when active (`showAr` is `true` or when in `webxr` mode to allow entering AR) ensures the camera is only active when needed. Note that `arSceneEnabled` and its associated `$effect` block can be removed entirely as they are no longer needed.

```
			{#if arMode !== null && (showAr || arMode === "webxr")}
				<ARScene {arMode} onArrowAnchor={setArrowAnchor} onScene={setArScene} />
			{/if}
```

**6 issues found** across 19 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P2: Storage mock state leaks between tests: `localStorage` and `sessionStorage` values persist across test cases because the `beforeEach` only clears vi mocks. Individual tests currently work around this with manual `localStorage.clear()` calls, but the setup should handle it globally for consistency and to prevent flaky test order dependencies. P1: `three-bmfont-text` override replaces A-Frame's dmarcos fork with the upstream npm package. A-Frame depends on `github:dmarcos/three-bmfont-text#eed487...` (Diego Marcos's fork with A-Frame-specific patches). Overriding to `3.0.1` pulls the Jam3 upstream version instead, which may have API differences or missing patches that could break text/SVG rendering in A-Frame. If the intent is to lock the fork, use `"three-bmfont-text": "github:dmarcos/three-bmfont-text#eed4878795be9b3e38cf6aec6b903f56acd1f695"` or verify the override doesn't break the AR scene. P2: The `direction-arrow` A-Frame component creates Three.js geometries and materials on init but never disposes them on remove. When the AR scene re-initializes or the entity is detached, these GPU resources accumulate without release. Under sustained use (navigating in/out of AR view, switching holes) this can lead to observable GPU memory growth. Adding a `remove: function () { … }` handler that calls `.dispose()` on the material and each geometry, then `this.el.removeObject3D("mesh")`, would match the standard Three.js/A-Frame disposal pattern. P1: If the `import("maplibre-gl")` dynamic import ever fails (transient network blip, ad-blocker interference, CDN stall), the rejected promise is permanently cached in `maplibreLoad`. The `??=` operator never replaces it, so every subsequent call to `loadMapLibre()` returns that same rejected promise, and `maplibregl` stays null. In `MapGame.svelte`, the `watchPosition` callback retries `initializeMap(point)` when `map` is still null, but it hits the cached failure every time — the map never recovers without a full page reload. The fix is to catch the rejection inside the promise chain and reset `maplibreLoad = null` so a retry can attempt the import again. P1: This condition keeps `` mounted after `arSceneEnabled` flips to true, even when AR is no longer visible in `arjs` mode. Mounting it only while `showAr` is active (while still allowing `webxr`) prevents background camera usage. P2: If the first dynamic import fails, `maplibreLoad` holds a rejected promise and every later call returns the same failure immediately. Resetting `maplibreLoad` on rejection allows retrying after transient load/network errors. ```
Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix.

P1: If the `import("maplibre-gl")` dynamic import ever fails (transient network blip, ad-blocker interference, CDN stall), the rejected promise is permanently cached in `maplibreLoad`. The `??=` operator never replaces it, so every subsequent call to `loadMapLibre()` returns that same rejected promise, and `maplibregl` stays null. In `MapGame.svelte`, the `watchPosition` callback retries `initializeMap(point)` when `map` is still null, but it hits the cached failure every time — the map never recovers without a full page reload. The fix is to catch the rejection inside the promise chain and reset `maplibreLoad = null` so a retry can attempt the import again.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At src/lib/MapGame.svelte, line 102:

<comment>If the `import("maplibre-gl")` dynamic import ever fails (transient network blip, ad-blocker interference, CDN stall), the rejected promise is permanently cached in `maplibreLoad`. The `??=` operator never replaces it, so every subsequent call to `loadMapLibre()` returns that same rejected promise, and `maplibregl` stays null. In `MapGame.svelte`, the `watchPosition` callback retries `initializeMap(point)` when `map` is still null, but it hits the cached failure every time — the map never recovers without a full page reload. The fix is to catch the rejection inside the promise chain and reset `maplibreLoad = null` so a retry can attempt the import again.</comment>

</file context>
```

```suggestion
	maplibreLoad ??= Promise.all([
		import("maplibre-gl"),
		import("maplibre-gl/dist/maplibre-gl.css"),
	]).then(([module]) => {
		maplibregl = module;
		return module;
	}).catch((error) => {
		maplibreLoad = null;
		throw error;
	});
```

P2: The `direction-arrow` A-Frame component creates Three.js geometries and materials on init but never disposes them on remove. When the AR scene re-initializes or the entity is detached, these GPU resources accumulate without release. Under sustained use (navigating in/out of AR view, switching holes) this can lead to observable GPU memory growth. Adding a `remove: function () { … }` handler that calls `.dispose()` on the material and each geometry, then `this.el.removeObject3D("mesh")`, would match the standard Three.js/A-Frame disposal pattern.

When the AR scene re-initializes or the entity is detached, these GPU resources accumulate without release. Under sustained use (navigating in/out of AR view, switching holes) this can lead to observable GPU memory growth. Adding a `remove: function () { … }` handler that calls `.dispose()` on the material and each geometry, then `this.el.removeObject3D("mesh")`, would match the standard Three.js/A-Frame disposal pattern.</comment>
